title: festuca elatior subsp. pratensis
intro: Festuca elatior subsp. pratensis, also known as meadow fescue, is a subspecies of a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Festuca elatior subsp. pratensis include its potential to aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
